prs.title: Add logBreadcrumb to AnalyticsHelper for PostHog breadcrumb trail
prs.body:
## What
Adds a public `AnalyticsHelper.logBreadcrumb(_:category:properties:)` that captures a `breadcrumb` event to PostHog, building a labeled trail of user actions/screens (visible in the Activity feed and as markers in session replay).

## Why
The app (`CallRecorder`) PR [#201](https://github.com/sagar2305/CallRecorder/pull/201) adds breadcrumb logging at key user flows (app launch, home shown, outgoing call, recording playback, transcription start/finish, subscription screen). That PR depends on this API, which lives in this package — without this change the app will not compile.

## Implementation
- Uses the same `PostHogSDK.shared.capture(...)` path as existing `logEvent` calls — no new dependency, no new config.
- `category` defaults to `"navigation"`; `message` and `category` are folded into the event properties.
- Doc comment warns against passing PII (phone numbers, recording URLs) in breadcrumbs.
- Adds a unit test exercising the new API with the message/category/properties shapes used at the app call sites.

## Versioning
This is additive, backward-compatible new public API → minor bump. After merge, tag **`1.1.0`** (current latest tag is `1.0.0`) so the app can pin it.

## Verification
Built for iOS Simulator (iPhone 16 Pro, Xcode 26.5). `AnalyticsHelper.swift` compiles cleanly. Note: the build surfaces a **pre-existing, unrelated** error in `SKProductExtensions.swift:12` (`SubscriptionPeriod` ambiguous between StoreKit's new iOS 26.5 type and RevenueCat's) — that file is untouched by this PR and fails the same way on `main`.

prs.title: fix: image-gen retry on empty response + reconcile recency window
prs.body:
Follow-up to #10 (merged after these two changes were pushed). Two more reliability fixes.

## 1. Image generation retry (`scripts/generate_images.ts`)
`generateImage()` now retries when Gemini returns **200 OK but no image part** (`No image data in response`). This was thrown immediately and aborted the entire cycle; it's transient (Gemini sometimes returns only a text part), so it now retries like the existing 5xx/network branches.

## 2. Reconcile recency window (`scripts/reconcile_failed.ts`)
Added `--max-age-hours` (default 24h). Only failures within the window are re-posted, so stale already-resolved `error` rows can't be re-posted into duplicates.

## Verification
- `tsc --noEmit` clean
- Full draft-mode cycle ran end-to-end (all slides generated, draft submitted)
- Reconcile dry-run confirmed old failures are now skipped, recent ones kept

prs.title: feat: post reliability — Gemini retry + auto-reconcile failed posts
prs.body:
Follow-up to #9. Two reliability improvements for the posting pipeline, both addressing real production failures.

## 1. Retry Gemini content generation (`scripts/text_overlay.ts`)
`generateAIContent` now retries up to 3 times on a 5xx, empty body, or invalid/truncated JSON (`Expected ',' or '}'`) instead of returning null on the first flaky response. A single bad Gemini response was aborting the whole cycle — worst for RoastAI, which has no CSV fallback.

## 2. Auto-reconcile failed posts (`scripts/reconcile_failed.ts`, `npm run reconcile`)
Some posts fail **asynchronously**: they submit fine (tracker = `pending`/`scheduled`), then Blotato marks them `failed` during publishing — the transient `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'status')` TikTok hiccup (confirmed by Blotato support: the platform returned no response; the fix is to retry). `retry_handler.ts` only catches synchronous submit failures, so these needed daily manual re-posting.

`reconcile_failed.ts` treats Blotato as source of truth:
- reads the active campaign's POST-TRACKER non-terminal rows
- queries `GET /v2/posts/:id` for each
- for failed **+ retryable** ones, re-posts the archived slides directly (no Gemini regeneration), bumps the archive attempts counter (cap = `MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS`), and marks the original row `retried → <new-id>`
- correctly classifies non-retryable failures (e.g. "maximum 3 unique accounts" plan limit) and leaves them alone

Campaign-aware (own `dataPath`-based row marker; resolves account index from the live campaign-scoped config so a roastai run never touches minutewise archives). Includes `--dry-run`.

## Verification
- `tsc --noEmit` clean
- `npm run reconcile --dry-run` validated on both campaigns (correctly identified retryable vs non-retryable, respected attempt cap, handled missing archives gracefully)
- Full draft-mode cycle run end-to-end: content → images → Blotato draft, caption with body + 20 hashtags

prs.title: feat: richer captions (body text) + ~18 hashtags from the bank
prs.body:
## What & why
Posts were shipping a bare hook + 4–5 hashtags. Two fixes in `scripts/text_overlay.ts`:

- **Richer captions** — the caption now includes a 2–4 sentence body. The Gemini prompt emits a `caption` field, threaded through `generateAIContent` → `generateContent` → `buildCaption()`. CSV-fallback posts synthesize a body from the slide copy.
- **More hashtags (~18)** — `pickHashtags()` now actually uses `HASHTAG-BANK.md` (the `_hashtagBank` arg was previously ignored): parses the Top Performers table and fills `branded + tracked + trending + bank` up to a target of 18, deduped case-insensitively. A `BANK_DENYLIST` drops off-topic entity tags (`#nba`, `#psg`, …). Fixes the CLAUDE.md "20 vs actual 4–5" mismatch and lets campaigns with few branded tags (RoastAI: 3) still reach ~18.

## Verification
Ran a live test across 3 accounts at now / 3 PM / 5 PM. **8/9 posts published** with full captions + 18 tags. The single failure was an unrelated TikTok per-account rate-limit (other accounts at the same time succeeded; same account's later post succeeded), not a code issue.

prs.title: fix: full project audit — 33 bug fixes (security, reliability, data integrity)
prs.body:
## Summary

Full security & reliability audit of the entire project (dashboard + engine + frontend). Found and fixed **33 bugs** across 25 files, organized by severity.

### Critical Fixes (7)
- **Scheduler race condition** — two ticks could claim the same batch simultaneously, spawning duplicate cycles and creating duplicate TikTok posts. Fixed with atomic `UPDATE ... WHERE last_run_date != today` claim.
- **Partial slide uploads posted** — if one slide upload failed mid-batch, remaining slides were posted as a shorter slideshow. Now aborts the entire post if any upload returns empty.
- **Division by zero in optimizer** — both experiment variants with 0 views caused `Infinity > 0.2 = true`, declaring meaningless winners. Added minimum 50-views threshold.
- **Zombie processes** — spawned child processes had no timeout; hung Gemini calls accumulated indefinitely. Added 2-hour kill timer.
- **Cascade deletes without error checks** — `deleteCreator()` ran 3 cascade operations without checking errors, risking partial corruption. All operations now checked.
- **Failed posts invisible to retry handler** — failed submissions were never written to POST-TRACKER.md. Now tracked with `status=error`.
- **Temp files orphaned on cancellation** — cycle cancellation skipped cleanup. Fixed by hoisting `allTempFiles` and calling `cleanup()` in cancel handler.

### High-Priority Fixes (8)
- **Payout precision** — `dollarsToCents("19.995")` returned 1999 instead of 2000. Fixed with `toFixed(2)` before multiply.
- **Payment reference silently ignored** — `markPayoutPaid` idempotency key didn't include the reference; retries with different refs were silently accepted. Now updates reference on retry.
- **Creator account ownership update** — no error check after updating `owned_account_ids`. Added.
- **Gemini timeout** — no timeout on Gemini fetch calls; hung cycles blocked indefinitely. Added `AbortSignal.timeout(90000)`.
- **No error boundaries** — PostDrawerMount and NotificationsBell mounted globally without error boundaries; a crash took down the entire dashboard. Wrapped in ErrorBoundary.
- **`target_posts_per_week=0` impossible** — `Number("0") || 3` returned 3. Fixed with explicit null check.
- **Env vars not validated** — missing API keys caused cryptic runtime errors. Added `requireEnv()` that fails fast with clear message.
- **Campaign flow selection ignored by engine** — dashboard saved `flows_enabled` but engine always ran all 3 flows. Now reads from DB when no `--flow=` CLI flag is passed.

### Medium-Priority Fixes (18)
- Blotato 429 retry with exponential backoff
- Command injection risk in emoji shell args → now via env var
- TOCTOU race in experiment log writes → atomic write
- Raw images not cleaned on overlay failure → `finally` block
- Invalid CTA image used silently → Content-Type validation
- Emoji flow slide count mismatch → `.slice(0, 6)` cap
- Duplicate milestone alerts on DB insert failure → dedup fix
- Invalid hook styles propagate from optimizer → validation
- Metrics decrease without warning → logging added
- Promise rejections in UserNav/AvatarLink → `.catch()` added
- Double-submit on creator form → guard added
- Object URL memory leak on export failure → `try/finally`
- Auth crash when profile row missing → `.maybeSingle()`
- Stale campaign fallback silent → `console.warn()` when triggered
- `email_include` field not editable → checkboxes added to edit form
- Resource URL parsing broke on `|` in query params → ` | ` separator
- Auth checks missing on style training actions → `assertRole("admin")`
- Stale auth cookies on public pages → proxy clears expired `sb-` cookies

### New Feature: Milestone Alerts
- Posts crossing view thresholds now fire Slack/Discord webhook notifications
- Dedup via `milestone_alerts_fired` table (migration included + already applied to Supabase)

### New Files
- `dashboard/src/components/error-boundary.tsx`
- `dashboard/migrations/add_milestone_alerts_fired.sql`

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` — zero errors (engine + dashboard)
- [x] `npx next build` — all routes compile
- [x] Dev server responds on all pages
- [x] Engine arg parsing verified with `TEST_PARSE_ONLY=1`
- [ ] Campaign edit → save → verify email_include checkboxes persist
- [ ] Payout approve → mark paid flow
- [ ] Scheduler concurrent tick test

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

prs.body:
## Summary

- **Multi-campaign CMS** — every Signup Control page (Brief, TikTok Setup, Auth Form, Brand Theme) can now be edited per campaign. Migration adds a `campaign` column + composite primary key to `cms_pages` / `cms_page_versions`. The shared `welcome` row is pinned to a `_shared` sentinel.
- **Explicit "publish to chooser" workflow** — new `campaigns.show_on_chooser` boolean. Admin adds a campaign → it's visible in `/signup-control` only. Admin clicks the Show / Hide toggle on the tile → the campaign appears on the public `/welcome/campaign` chooser. The 3 originally-shipped apps (Minutewise, Roast AI, Call Recorder) are backfilled to visible.
- **Mobile-responsive shell + content** — dashboard sidebar collapses to a hamburger drawer below `md`; campaigns Gantt swaps for a vertical card list on phone; TikTok setup step header restructured so the "Mark as Complete" button doesn't squeeze the title; chooser tiles are 2-per-row on phone and 3-per-row on tablet+; creator portal nav drops to a second row on phone.

Two new SQL migrations under `dashboard/migrations/`:

1. `add_cms_campaign_column.sql` — multi-campaign extension for the CMS tables.
2. `add_campaign_show_on_chooser.sql` — `show_on_chooser` boolean + backfill for the 3 default campaigns.

prs.title: feat: creator self-signup onboarding + admin application editor
prs.body:
## Summary

End-to-end **prospective-creator onboarding flow** (anonymous → brief → setup → signup → linked creator) plus an **admin-side editor** for the application data collected at signup.

## What's in this PR

### Public onboarding (no auth)
- **`/creator/brief`** — 12-section internship pitch (hero, about us, intern showcases, compensation, FAQ, 7-step journey, CTAs)
- **`/creator/setup/tiktok`** — 6-step interactive TikTok account setup guide with real Loom/YouTube video embeds
- **Sequential step locking** — Step N pill + Next Step button disabled until previous step marked complete (Lock icon + tooltip + aria-label)
- **`/creator/login` + `/creator/signup`** — unified tabbed form (15 application fields, indigo theme, Brewapps logo)
- **Contextual error banners** on login for `?reason=otp_expired`, `not-a-creator`, `access_denied` with inline **"Resend confirmation email"** action
- **`src/proxy.ts`** — adds `/creator/brief` and `/creator/setup` to `publicRoutes` so anonymous visitors can reach the brief before the login wall (Next 16 renamed `middleware.ts` → `proxy.ts`)

### Linker (`src/lib/link-creator.ts`)
- Auto-creates the `creators` row on first sign-in when no admin pre-invite exists
- Required `kind: 'team_member'` NOT NULL column included
- Best-effort sync of application metadata from `auth.user_metadata` onto the new row (encrypts 4 password fields)

### Admin editor
- **Application Details card** on `/creators/[id]` grouped by platform (TikTok / YouTube / Instagram / Facebook) with colored icon badges
- 👁️ **Eye-icon reveal** decrypts password fields on demand via admin-gated `revealCreatorPassword` server action
- `updateCreator` whitelist extended with 11 new keys; 4 password fields `encryptSecret()`'d before write

### Auth callback hardening
- `/auth/callback` now surfaces Supabase `error_code` as `?reason=` on the login page (was silently bouncing)
- `/auth/relink` logs unlinked reasons for diagnostics

### Schema
- `migrations/add_creator_application_columns.sql` adds 11 columns to `creators` (`phone`, `whatsapp`, `*_username`, `*_password_enc`, `*_email`, `facebook_url`). Idempotent (`IF NOT EXISTS`).

## Test plan
- [x] `/welcome` → click **Creator** card → `/creator/brief` renders
- [x] Brief → **Get Started** → `/creator/setup/tiktok` shows Step 1 active, Steps 2-6 locked
- [x] Mark Step 1 complete → Step 2 unlocks, Next Step button enables (gated)
- [x] Walk through Steps 2-6 → Finish Setup → `/creator/signup`
- [x] Fill 15-field signup form → submit → Supabase confirmation email
- [x] Confirm email → `/auth/callback` → linker auto-creates creators row → land on `/creator` portal
- [x] Admin (`/login`) → Creators list → open a creator → see Application Details card
- [x] Edit a field + click Save → persists; reload → values stay
- [x] Click 👁️ on a password field → decrypted value shown
- [x] Sign in with unconfirmed email → see amber banner with "Resend confirmation email"
- [x] Click resend → new email sent (rate-limit aware)
- [x] TypeScript: 0 errors • ESLint: 0 warnings with `--max-warnings 0`
- [x] All 6 public routes serve HTTP 200; all 4 gated routes 307 anon users

## Operator notes

⚠️ **Run the migration** in Supabase Studio → SQL Editor before deploying this branch. The `Save` action on the Application Details card requires the new columns.

⚠️ This PR ships with `RLS off` assumption on the `creators` table for the auto-create flow. If RLS is enabled, add a service-role key (`SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`) and route the linker through it, or write an INSERT policy keyed on `auth.jwt() ->> 'email'`.

prs.title: fix: Comprehensive Production Audit — 47 Core Bugs & React 19 Compliance Resolved
prs.body:
###  Overview
This Pull Request delivers a comprehensive, production-grade system cleanup across the entire repository following a multi-round architecture and code-quality audit. A total of **47 structural, functional, and compliance issues** have been successfully resolved.

Both the root project and dashboard now pass strict type-checking (`tsc --noEmit`) and ESLint compilation with zero errors and zero warnings (`--max-warnings 0`).

---

###  Key Changes Breakdown

#### 1. Core Automated Engine & Data Infrastructure
* **A/B Experimentation:** Fixed the broken dead-branch ternary logic in `optimizer.ts:205` that was logging all tests as a generic `'keep'` state.
* **Telemetry & State Stability:** Fixed a critical silent telemetry null value that risked letting the operator trigger accidental duplicate posting cycles.
* **Race Conditions:** Implemented an atomic utility wrapper (`atomicWrite`) to resolve concurrent read-mutate-write collisions on shared file logs.
* **Error Resilience:** Patched unhandled exceptions where Gemini API JSON responses would conflict with shape models.

#### 2. Dashboard Interface & React 19 Strict-Mode Alignment
* **Hydration & State Sync:** Resolved `Date.now()` layout-flicker anomalies and unmounted `setState` execution routines.
* **Impure Side-Effects:** Refactored 14 core components to prevent cascading synchronous state dispatches inside hook structures, adhering completely to React 19 purity compliance.
* **Component Optimization:** Stabilized form inputs from losing focus mid-keystroke.

#### 3. Financial Mechanics & Data Security
* **Access Control:** Deployed strict administrative authority validators (`assertRole(\"admin\")`) across 5 sensitive system mutation endpoints.
* **Payout Reliability:** Eradicated silent Unix-epoch defaults (Jan 1, 1970) on missing post dates inside production payout routines. Fixed negative balance flags.

#### 4. Production Integration & Media Handling
* **Payload Compaction:** Configured API endpoints to filter out raw HTML stream data dumps into cleanly formatted summary definitions.
* **Backoff Strategy:** Implemented a robust incremental delayed timeout algorithm (15s/20s/25s) to protect image generation pipes.

---

# Description

This Pull Request implements an architectural refactoring of the Tasks router in [src/routers/openml/tasks.py](cci:7://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Esoc/server-api/src/routers/openml/tasks.py:0:0-0:0). ### Key Changes:
- **Database Abstraction**: Added a new [get_lookup_data](cci:1://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Esoc/server-api/src/database/tasks.py:119:0-131:42) function in [src/database/tasks.py](cci:7://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Esoc/server-api/src/database/tasks.py:0:0-0:0) to handle dynamic table lookups required by task templates.

- **Security Maintenance**: Preserved and integrated the strict table whitelisting for `LOOKUP` directives to ensure the system remains secure against SQL injection.

prs.title: security: implement SQL parameterization and table whitelisting in da…
prs.body:
## Changes
This Pull Request addresses critical **SQL Injection (SQLi)** vulnerabilities identified in the `datasets.py` and `tasks.py` routers. The changes replace insecure raw string concatenation with secure SQL parameterization and implement strict table whitelisting.

**Key Changes:**
- **SQL Parameterization:** Refactored `list_datasets` in `src/routers/openml/datasets.py` to use SQLAlchemy `text()` and `bindparam`. This includes correctly handling list parameters with `expanding=True` for `IN` clauses to prevent injection via tags or IDs.

prs.title: [BUG] Fix unreliable local scope in _materialize by using explicit namespace.
prs.body:
LLM generated content, by Gemini 3 Flash

## Change
This PR fixes a scoping bug in the `_materialize` method of `_AiodModelPkg` within `src/aiod/models/base/_base.py`. 

The previous implementation relied on `exec(self._obj)` to update the local function scope, which is unreliable in Python 3.x and often leads to `NameError` because the dynamically defined `obj` variable isn't captured. 

**Improvements:**
- Introduced an explicit `namespace` dictionary to capture variables during `exec`.
- Replaced the direct variable return with `namespace.get("obj")` for safe retrieval.
- Removed the `# noqa: F821` linting bypass as the variable is now accessed through a dictionary, making the code cleaner and more robust.

## How to Test
The fix can be verified by running the existing model materialization tests:
1. Activate environment: `source .venv/bin/activate`
2. Run targeted tests: `python -m pytest tests/test_models.py` (or the relevant model test file).
3. Confirm that `materialize()` no longer throws `NameError` in environments where local scope optimization was causing failures.

prs.title: feat: add project templates for pixi init
prs.body:
This PR adds support for project templates to the `pixi init` command. Users can now use the `--template` (or `-t`) flag to bootstrap projects with pre-configured dependencies and tasks for specific ecosystems.

**Supported Templates:**
- `python`: Adds `python` and a `start` task.
- `rust`: Adds `rust` and `build`/`run` tasks.
- `cpp`: Adds a C++ toolchain (`gcc`, `cmake`, `ninja`) and build/configure tasks.

prs.body:
## Description

This PR improves type safety for RxJS interoperability in Svelte stores by removing the `@ts-expect-error` suppression and properly typing stores that return RxJS-style subscriptions.

### Problem

The `subscribe_to_store` utility function in `packages/svelte/src/store/utils.js` had a TODO comment with `@ts-expect-error` to handle RxJS-style stores that return `{ unsubscribe: () => void }` instead of a plain function. This suppressed type errors but didn't provide proper type safety.

### Solution

- **Added `InteropReadable<T>` interface** in `packages/svelte/src/store/private.d.ts` to properly type stores that use the RxJS subscription pattern
- **Removed `@ts-expect-error` suppression** and replaced it with a proper runtime check using the `in` operator
- **Added comprehensive tests** to verify both RxJS-style and Svelte-style store subscriptions work correctly
- **Maintained full backward compatibility** with existing Svelte stores

### Changes

- `packages/svelte/src/store/private.d.ts`: Added `InteropReadable` interface
- `packages/svelte/src/store/utils.js`: Improved type safety with proper runtime checks
- `packages/svelte/tests/store-utils.test.ts`: Added tests for both subscription styles
- `.changeset/eleven-pears-ask.md`: Added changeset for patch version

```bash

Both tests verify that the cleanup function is called correctly.

## Description

This PR addresses the issue of silent failures when browser autoplay policies block media playback triggered via `bind:paused`.

prs.title: Fix: Goroutine leaks in SQL/Redis datasources by adding graceful shut…
prs.body:
# Issue No.  #2898 

## Description

**What:** Implemented graceful shutdown for background goroutines in SQL and Redis datasources.

**Why:** Previously, the goroutines responsible for connection retries (`retryConnection`) and metrics collection (`pushDBMetrics`) ran in infinite loops that did not respect the application's shutdown signal. This caused goroutine leaks and persistent error logging even after the application stopped.

**How:**
- Added a `stopSignal` channel and `sync.Once` to `DB` and `Redis` structs.
- Updated the `Close()` methods to safely close this channel.
- Modified the background loops to listen for `<-stopSignal` and exit immediately upon shutdown.

**Benefits:** Ensures cleaner application shutdown, frees up resources, and eliminates confusing log errors after the app has stopped.

---

## Breaking Changes (if applicable)

- None. This PR only improves the internal shutdown logic of the datasources and does not change any public APIs.

---

## Additional Information

- This change affects `pkg/gofr/datasource/sql` and `pkg/gofr/datasource/redis`.
- No new dependencies were added.

---

prs.title: fix(insertMany): surface per-document validation errors for unordered inserts
prs.body:
Summary

Unordered insertMany() silently dropped per-document validation errors unless rawResult: true was set, so users couldn’t tell which documents failed. This change decorates every unordered outcome (empty arrays, successful document arrays, and Mongo bulk errors) with mongoose.validationErrors/mongoose.results, ensuring parity with the rawResult path. Targeted regression tests have been added to lock down this new behavior.

Examples

The new tests in test/model.insertMany.test.js demonstrate that await Model.insertMany([...], { ordered: false }) now returns the successful documents plus docs.mongoose.validationErrors listing the failed indexes. A failing unordered insert (MongoBulkWriteError) now carries the same mongoose.validationErrors/results metadata even when rawResult is false.

This pull request addresses issue https://github.com/Automattic/mongoose/issues/15771.

issues.title: Encrypt creator credentials on signup (currently in auth user_metadata as plaintext)
issues.body:
**Pre-existing security item flagged by CodeRabbit during review of PR #5.** Predates the CMS work in that PR — the signup flow has always written plaintext social-media passwords into Supabase Auth's \`user_metadata\`.

## What's wrong today

In \`dashboard/src/components/creator-auth-form.tsx\` (around lines 327-340), the creator signup flow does:

\`\`\`ts
sb.auth.signUp({
  email: form.personalEmail,
  password: form.dashboardPassword,
  options: {
    data: {
      full_name: form.fullName,
      tiktok_password: form.tiktokPassword,           // ← plaintext
      tiktok_email_password: form.tiktokEmailPassword,// ← plaintext
      youtube_password: form.youtubePassword,         // ← plaintext
      instagram_password: form.instagramPassword,     // ← plaintext
      // ...
    },
  },
});
\`\`\`

Everything in \`options.data\` lands in \`auth.users.user_metadata\` as plaintext. Anyone with Supabase Studio admin access or the service-role key can read all creator social-media passwords.

The downstream pipeline does encrypt these into \`creators.*_enc\` columns (via \`encryptSecret\` / \`credential-vault\`), but the **unencrypted copy in \`user_metadata\` is never cleared**.

## Proposed fix (separate PR)

1. **Stop sending secrets to \`signUp\` \`options.data\`.** Only non-sensitive profile fields go to \`user_metadata\`.
2. **New server endpoint** \`POST /api/creator-credentials\` (or server action) that:
   - Accepts the credentials over HTTPS from the signup form _after_ signUp succeeds
   - Calls \`assertRole\` for the just-created user
   - Encrypts per-field and writes into the \`creators\` row keyed by the new user id
3. **Backfill** for existing rows: walk \`auth.users\` where \`user_metadata\` contains those keys, encrypt them into \`creators.*_enc\` if not already populated, then clear the plaintext entries from \`user_metadata\` via \`admin.updateUserById\`.

## Threat model

A leaked service-role key or a compromised Supabase Studio session today exposes every creator's TikTok/YouTube/Instagram passwords in plaintext. After the fix it'd expose only encrypted ciphertext that requires the \`CREDENTIAL_VAULT_KEY\` env var to decrypt.

## Existing primitives to reuse

- \`dashboard/src/lib/credential-vault.ts\` — already has \`encryptSecret\` / \`decryptSecret\`.
- \`dashboard/migrations/add_creator_application_columns.sql\` — already defines the \`creators.*_enc\` columns we'd write to.

cc: original flag from CodeRabbit during review of #5.

issues.title: [Security] SQL Injection Vulnerability in datasets and tasks routers
issues.body:
### **Describe the bug**
A critical security vulnerability was identified in the `list_datasets` and task lookup functions within the routers. The application was using raw string concatenation (f-strings) to build SQL queries from user-provided input. 

This creates a high-risk **SQL Injection (SQLi)** vulnerability, allowing a malicious user to manipulate the query structure and execute unauthorized database commands, leading to potential data loss or unauthorized access.

### **To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Send a request to the `/datasets/list` endpoint.
2. Provide a `tag` parameter containing malicious SQL syntax, for example: 
   `'study_14'); DROP TABLE dataset; --`
3. **Result:** The server concatenates this string directly into the query, which would attempt to drop the `dataset` table.

### **Expected behavior**
User input must always be treated as data, never as part of the SQL command structure. All database interactions should:
- Use **parameterized placeholders** (Bind Parameters).
- Strictly validate any dynamic table or column names against a predefined **whitelist**.

### **Additional context**
This vulnerability was found in the following files:
- `src/routers/openml/datasets.py`
- `src/routers/openml/tasks.py`

**Status:** A fix has already been implemented in the branch `feature/sql-parameterization` and is ready for review.

issues.title: [MNT] Deprecation of datetime.utcnow() and improvement of synchronization logic
issues.body:
### **Describe the bug / Optimization**
Currently, the codebase uses `datetime.utcnow()` and `datetime.utcfromtimestamp()` in several core modules. As of Python 3.12, these methods are officially deprecated and can lead to bugs because they return "naive" datetime objects (without timezone info). 

Additionally, the current error handling and synchronization logic in `synchronization.py` and `error_handling.py` use legacy methods like `traceback.print_exc()`, which are less effective for production logging.

### **Proposed Changes**
I propose a modernization of the time handling and logging logic:

1. **Datetime Modernization:** Replace all instances of `utcnow()` and `utcfromtimestamp()` with timezone-aware `datetime.now(UTC)` in:
   - `aiod_entry.py`
   - `bookmark.py`
   - Routers and Connectors.
2. **Standardized Logging:** Update `error_handling.py` to use `logging.exception()` for better traceability.
3. **Synchronization Refactoring:** Use the `csv` module in `synchronization.py` to make error reporting more robust and remove redundant logic.
4. **Test Updates:** Update `test_bookmark_endpoints.py` to ensure compatibility with timezone-aware objects.

### **Expected impact**
- Future-proof code that is compatible with Python 3.12+.
- More reliable timestamp comparisons in tests and production.
- Better error visibility in logs.

### **Additional context**
I have already verified these changes locally, and all 30 relevant tests in `src/tests/` are passing. I am ready to submit a PR for this!

issues.title: [Bug]:Fix scoping bug in model deserialization using dynamic code execution
issues.body:
### **Describe the bug**
In the `_materialize` method of `_AiodModelPkg` (located in `src/aiod/models/base/_base.py`), the SDK uses `exec(self._obj)` to dynamically reconstruct objects from code strings. 

However, in Python 3.x, calling `exec` inside a function without providing an explicit dictionary for locals does not reliably update the function's local scope. This causes the variable `obj` (which is expected to be defined by the execution of `self._obj`) to either raise a `NameError` or behave unpredictably during the `return obj` statement.

### **To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create an `_AiodModelPkg` instance where `self._obj` contains a code string defining `obj`.
2. Call the `.materialize()` method.
3. **Result:** The call may fail with `NameError: name 'obj' is not defined` because the dynamic variable was not captured in the function's local namespace.

### **Expected behavior**
The `exec` call should use an explicit namespace dictionary to capture defined variables safely. The method should then retrieve `obj` from that specific namespace to ensure consistent behavior across all Python environments and versions.

### **Proposed Solution**
Refactor the `_materialize` method to use an explicit namespace:
```python
namespace = {}
exec(self._obj, globals(), namespace)
return namespace.get("obj")

issues.title: [Bug]:Fix inconsistent error handling and JSON decode errors in API calls (aiod/calls.py).
issues.body:
### **Describe the bug**
Currently, the API methods in `src/aiod/calls/calls.py` handle server responses inconsistently, leading to unpredictable SDK behavior:

1. **Silent Error Swallowing:** Methods like `post_asset`, `put_asset`, `patch_asset`, and `delete_asset` "swallow" non-200 HTTP errors (e.g., 400 or 500). Instead of raising an exception, they return the raw `requests.Response` object, which breaks the expected return type (usually a string identifier).
2. **Unhandled Exceptions:** Methods like `get_list` and `search` attempt to call `res.json()` immediately without checking `res.ok`. If the server returns a non-JSON error (like an HTML 500 page), the SDK crashes with a `JSONDecodeError`.

### **To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior in the current code:
1. Trigger a server-side error (500) while calling `post_asset`. 
   - **Result:** The function returns the `<Response [500]>` object instead of raising an error.
2. Call `get_list` on an endpoint that returns a plain-text error message.
   - **Result:** The SDK crashes abruptly with `json.decoder.JSONDecodeError`.

### **Expected behavior**
Every API method in `calls.py` should consistently call `res.raise_for_status()` immediately after receiving a response. This ensures that a `requests.exceptions.HTTPError` is properly raised for any non-2xx responses before the SDK attempts to process the payload.

### **Proposed Solution**
- Implement `res.raise_for_status()` across all methods in `src/aiod/calls/calls.py`.
- Standardize return types in method annotations to ensure predictable output for downstream clients.

### **Additional context**
I have already implemented these fixes locally and verified that HTTP errors are now correctly raised as exceptions. I will be opening a Pull Request shortly!

issues.title: [MAINT] Optimize test_fh.py by replacing slow estimators.

In `sktime/forecasting/base/tests/test_fh.py`, there are three legacy tests marked with the comment: 
`# TODO: Replace this long running test with fast unit test`. 

The following tests instantiate computationally heavy estimators (AutoARIMA, ExponentialSmoothing, etc.) just to verify the outputs of `ForecastingHorizon`, which unnecessarily slows down the CI pipeline:

**Benefits:**

- **Efficiency:** Replaces heavy model fitting with a lightweight `NaiveForecaster` without losing functional coverage of `ForecastingHorizon` logic.
- **Code Health:** Addresses and resolves long-standing `TODO` markers in the codebase.

issues.title: [ENH] Make missing alpha error message in PinballLoss more informative.
issues.body:
### **Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Currently, when evaluating the `PinballLoss` metric with an `alpha` parameter that is missing from the predictions (`y_pred`), it raises a generic `ValueError`: 
> `"not all quantile values in alpha are available in y_pred"`

There is an active `TODO` comment in `skpro/metrics/_classes.py` acknowledging this:
`# todo: make error msg more informative - which alphas are missing`

This can be frustrating for users as they have to manually debug which expected quantiles are missing from their forecasted DataFrame.

### **Describe the solution you'd like**

I'd like the error message to explicitly list the specific `alpha` values that are missing. The `PinballLoss._evaluate_by_index` method should compute the set difference between the requested `alpha` and the provided `y_pred_alphas` and include them in the exception.

* **Old Error Message:** `ValueError: "not all quantile values in alpha are available in y_pred"`
* **New Error Message:** `ValueError: "not all quantile values in alpha are available in y_pred. Missing alphas: [0.1, 0.9]"`

### **Describe alternatives you've considered**

Leaving the error message as-is was considered, but calculating the missing values using `set(alpha) - set(y_pred_alphas)` has minimal overhead and significantly improves the developer experience when debugging probabilistic pipelines.

### **Additional context**

I have already implemented this fix locally and updated `test_evaluate_alpha_negative` in `test_probabilistic_metrics.py` to verify the new error message format using `pytest.raises`. I am ready to submit a PR for this!

issues.title: test: add error case, display roundtrip, and serde roundtrip tests for EntryPoint
issues.body:
## Problem

The [EntryPoint](cci:2://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Esoc/retller/rattler2/crates/rattler_conda_types/src/package/entry_point.rs:21:0-30:1) struct in [crates/rattler_conda_types/src/package/entry_point.rs](cci:7://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Esoc/retller/rattler2/crates/rattler_conda_types/src/package/entry_point.rs:0:0-0:0) currently has limited test coverage. The existing test only validates successful parsing of two happy-path inputs. The following scenarios are untested:

- **Error handling** — No tests verify that malformed strings (missing `=`, missing `:`, empty input) are properly rejected
- **Display ↔ FromStr roundtrip** — No test confirms that formatting an [EntryPoint](cci:2://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Esoc/retller/rattler2/crates/rattler_conda_types/src/package/entry_point.rs:21:0-30:1) via `Display` and parsing it back via `FromStr` produces an identical result
- **Serde roundtrip** — No test verifies that the custom `Serialize`/`Deserialize` implementations correctly roundtrip through JSON

## Proposed Solution

issues.title: False positive error logs during graceful HTTP server shutdown.
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

When a GoFr application is gracefully shut down (e.g., via Ctrl+C or programmatically), the HTTP server's `ListenAndServe` or `ListenAndServeTLS` method returns `http.ErrServerClosed`.

Currently, the code in `pkg/gofr/http_server.go` logs this as an actual error:

"error while listening to http server, err: http: Server closed"

This is misleading because `http.ErrServerClosed` indicates a successful, intentional shutdown, not a failure.

---

**To Reproduce**

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. The code is

   ```go
   package main

   import "gofr.dev/pkg/gofr"

   func main() {
       app := gofr.New()
       app.Run()
   }

2. Run the application 
```go run main.go```

3. Stop it using Ctrl+C.
 
4. The error is ```ERRO [15:04:05] error while listening to http server, err: http: Server closed```




**Expected behavior**

The application should shut down cleanly without logging `"http: Server closed"` as an error.
This specific error return from `ListenAndServe` should be ignored or logged as an info message, as it is part of the normal shutdown lifecycle.


**Screenshots**
N/A

**Environments (please complete the following information):**
- OS: macOS
- gofr version: development
- go version: 1.25 

**More description**

I have identified the location in `pkg/gofr/http_server.go` (method `run`) where the error logging occurs.
I can modify the condition to explicitly ignore `http.ErrServerClosed` to prevent this strict error logging.

issues.title: Silent failure when parsing integer configuration values (MQTT/Kafka)
issues.body:
# Bug Report: Silent Integer Parsing Failures in GoFr Container

## Describe the bug

Several configuration values in `pkg/gofr/container/container.go` are parsed using `strconv.Atoi` where the returned error is explicitly ignored (assigned to `_`). If a user provides an invalid value (e.g., a non-numeric string or a value with units like `"500ms"`), the configuration silently defaults to `0` without any log or warning.

This affects:
- MQTT_PORT (Line 182)
- PARTITION_SIZE (Line 331)
- PUBSUB_OFFSET (Line 339)
- KAFKA_BATCH_SIZE (Line 340)
- KAFKA_BATCH_BYTES (Line 341)
- KAFKA_BATCH_TIMEOUT (Line 342)

For example, setting `KAFKA_BATCH_TIMEOUT="500ms"` results in a timeout of `0` (likely misinterpreted as 0ms or immediate timeout), whereas the intention was 500 milliseconds.

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Set an invalid integer value for one of the affected configurations in your `.env` file or environment variables:
   ```bash
   export MQTT_PORT="invalid_port"
   export KAFKA_BATCH_TIMEOUT="500ms"```


2. Initialize a GoFr application that uses these configurations (e.g., utilizing MQTT or Kafka PubSub).

3. The application starts without any error logs, but the port will be set to `0` and the timeout to `0`.


**Expected behavior**
The application should validate the integer configuration. If parsing fails, it should log a warning to inform the user that the provided value is invalid and state that it is falling back to the default value.


**Screenshots**
N/A

**Environments (please complete the following information):**
- OS:OS: macOS
- gofr version: development
- go version: 1.25

**More description**
I have identified the issue in `pkg/gofr/container/container.go` and can implement a helper function `getIntConfig` that handles the `strconv.Atoi` error by logging a warning and returning the default value.

issues.title: Refactor for Robustness: Fix Error Swallowing and Temp File Generation.
issues.body:
# CLI Improvements: Robustness & Better Error Handling

I've reviewed the codebase and identified a few areas where the current implementation could be **more robust** and **easier to debug**. This issue tracks the following improvements:

## 1. Fix Potential Crash in `getFilepath` (src/cli.ts)

**Problem:** Currently, `getFilepath` checks only two possible filenames (the default and one with a random ID). If by chance **both files exist**, the non-null assertion (`!`) at the end of the chain will cause the program to **crash**.

**Proposed Fix:**
- Implement a **loop** or **more robust retry mechanism** to guarantee a unique filename is always generated.

## 2. Improve Error Handling in `lstat` Helper (src/cli.ts)

**Problem:** The `lstat` helper currently **swallows all errors**. If a **permission error (EPERM)** or other system error occurs, it is treated indiscriminately as "file not found". This **masks actual system issues** that the user should know about.

**Proposed Fix:**
- Update the try-catch block to **only catch ENOENT errors** and **re-throw all others**.

## 3. Address Empty Catch Blocks in `kill` (src/core.ts)

**Problem:** The `kill` function contains multiple **empty try-catch blocks**. While ignoring errors when killing a non-existent process is valid, completely swallowing them makes **debugging difficult**.

**Proposed Fix:**
- Add **comments** explaining why these errors are ignored (e.g., `'ignore if process is already dead'`).
- Or **log them when verbose mode is enabled**.

issues.title: Resource Leak: Background goroutines in SQL and Redis datasources do not exit on Shutdown

labels.name: go
labels.description: Pull requests that update Go code

issues.body:
## **Describe the Bug**
The **SQL** and **Redis** datasource implementations spawn background goroutines for:
- Connection retries (`retryConnection`, `retryConnect`)
- Metrics collection (`pushDBMetrics`)

These goroutines run in **infinite loops** without monitoring a **cancellation context** or **stop signal**.

When the application shuts down (via `app.Shutdown()` or `container.Close()`), these goroutines **continue to run indefinitely**, resulting in:

- **Goroutine leaks** — resources are never freed.  
- **Log noise** — the application continues logging connection errors even after shutdown.

---

## **To Reproduce**

### **Steps to Reproduce**
1. Initialize a GoFr application with a SQL or Redis datasource configured to use an **unreachable host** (so the retry loop activates).
2. Start the application.
3. Trigger a shutdown (e.g., via `SIGINT`).
4. Observe that the background retry logic continues to execute and log errors even after shutdown.

### **Minimal Example**
```go
package main

import (
    "time"
    "gofr.dev/pkg/gofr"
)

func main() {
    app := gofr.New()
    
    // Configure a non-existent DB to force retry loop
    app.Config.Set("DB_HOST", "unreachable-host")
    app.Config.Set("DB_DIALECT", "mysql")
    
    go func() {
        time.Sleep(5 * time.Second)
        // Simulate shutdown
        // Even after this, the retryConnection goroutine keeps running
        app.Shutdown()
    }()
    
    app.Run()
} 
```


**Expected behavior**
When container.SQL.Close() or container.Redis.Close() is called (typically during app.Shutdown()),
all associated background goroutines (retry logic, metrics pushers, etc.) should:

Terminate immediately and gracefully.

Stop retrying connections or pushing metrics.

Release resources cleanly.

.

**Environments (please complete the following information):**
- OS: [macOS]
- gofr version [Latest source from development branch]
- go version [1.25 (as per go.mod)]

**More description**
- Add a `stopSignal` channel (and `sync.Once` for safety) to the **DB** and **Redis** structs.  
- Initialize this channel in `NewSQL` and `NewClient`.  
- Close this channel in the `Close()` method.  
- Update `retryConnection` and `pushDBMetrics` loops to listen for `<-stopSignal` and exit when triggered.

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
[DiagramContext.jsx](cci:7://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Ap%20merge%20project%20/drawdb/drawdb/src/context/DiagramContext.jsx:0:0-0:0) contained impure state updates where side effects (like updating the undo/redo stack) were occurring directly inside state setter functions. This can lead to unpredictable behavior, double-invocation in React Strict Mode, and state inconsistencies. Additionally, the context value was not memoized, causing unnecessary re-renders for all consuming components. [Workspace.jsx](cci:7://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Ap%20merge%20project%20/drawdb/drawdb/src/components/Workspace.jsx:0:0-0:0) also contained mixed concerns, handling both UI layout and complex data persistence logic.

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open [src/context/DiagramContext.jsx](cci:7://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Ap%20merge%20project%20/drawdb/drawdb/src/context/DiagramContext.jsx:0:0-0:0).
2. Observe [addTable](cci:1://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Ap%20merge%20project%20/drawdb/drawdb/src/context/DiagramContext.jsx:18:2-65:4), [deleteTable](cci:1://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Ap%20merge%20project%20/drawdb/drawdb/src/context/DiagramContext.jsx:67:2-105:4), etc., where [setUndoStack](cci:1://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Ap%20merge%20project%20/drawdb/drawdb/src/context/UndoRedoContext.jsx:4:2-4:24) is called inside `setTables` or `setRelationships` callbacks.

**Expected behavior**
- State updates should be pure. Side effects like updating history (undo/redo) should happen outside of state setters.
- Context values should be memoized to prevent performance degradation.
- Data persistence logic should be separated from UI components for better maintainability.

**Additional context**
I have implemented the following fixes:
- Refactored [DiagramContext.jsx](cci:7://file:///Users/mohitkourav/Desktop/Ap%20merge%20project%20/drawdb/drawdb/src/context/DiagramContext.jsx:0:0-0:0) to move side effects out of state updaters and added `useMemo` for the context value.

issues.title: Mitigate Timing Attacks in Authentication Middlewares & Add Input Validation Recommendations
issues.body: ## Describe the bug The Basic Authentication and API Key Authentication middlewares in GoFr previously used direct string equality for credential checks, exposing the framework and users to timing attack vulnerabilities [GoFr Authentication Guide](https://gofr.dev/docs/advanced-guide/http-authentication)[web:52][web:51]. Handler functions retrieve input parameters without automatic validation or sanitization, risking injection attacks and unexpected behavior. ## To Reproduce Steps to reproduce the behavior: The vulnerable code is (example for BasicAuth): return ok && storedPass == password // direct equality comparison The error is that timing attacks can leak information about valid credentials by measuring response times. ## Expected behavior Credential comparison should use constant-time comparison functions like `crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare` to prevent timing attacks. Handlers should validate and sanitize input parameters to ensure security and stability. ## Screenshots ## Environments (please complete the following information): - OS: Linux, macOS, Windows - gofr version: v1.x.x (latest) - go version: 1.21+ ## More description To fix this issue: - Updated authentication middleware functions in GoFr to use constant-time comparison for passwords and API keys. - Added dummy constant-time comparisons for non-existing credentials to maintain timing consistency. - Encouraged use of centralized input validation middleware/utilities for query, path, and body parameters. - Included recommendations for hash-based storage for passwords instead of plaintext. - Expanded test cases recommended to cover authentication logic and input validation edge cases. ## Links - [GoFr Official Site](https://gofr.dev/) - [GoFr GitHub Repository](https://github.com/gofr-dev/gofr)

issues.title: Security: Timing Attack Risk & Missing Input Validation in Auth Handlers
issues.body: ## Describe the bug Authentication middlewares (BasicAuth, APIKeyAuth) in GoFr ([go codebase](https://github.com/gofr-dev/gofr)) use direct string equality for credential checks, which exposes the framework and its adopters to timing attack vulnerabilities [web:52][web:51]. Handler functions (such as HelloHandler) retrieve input parameters using `Param` methods without automatic validation or sanitization, increasing the risk of injection and unexpected input errors. ## To Reproduce Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Review authentication code for credential comparison in middlewares: ``` if password == storedPassword { // direct comparison } ``` 2. Review HelloHandler or any typical handler for input handling: ``` name := c.Param("name") // only empty check, no sanitation/validation ``` ## Expected behavior - Credentials should be checked using constant-time comparison (`crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare`) for passwords and API keys. - Handlers should validate and sanitize all input parameters before use, ideally with reusable middleware or helper utilities. ## Screenshots Code example using constant-time comparison for authentication fix: ## Environments (please complete the following information): - OS: Linux, macOS, Windows (tested) - gofr version: v1.x.x (latest from [gofr.dev](https://gofr.dev/)) - go version: 1.21+ ## More description To fix: - Switch authentication credential checks in BasicAuth and APIKey middlewares to use constant-time comparison functions (see: [Go crypto/subtle documentation](https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/subtle)). - Add or encourage centralized input validation middleware/utilities for query, path, and body params. - Recommend use of hash-based password storage (not plaintext) for BasicAuth user config, following standard security practices [web:52][web:54]. - Strengthen and expand test cases for both authentication logic and input validation edge cases. Links for framework and official docs: - [GoFr official site](https://gofr.dev/) - [GoFr GitHub repository](https://github.com/gofr-dev/gofr)

labels.name: help

issues.body:

### Mongoose version

6.13.1

### Node.js version

14.21.3

### MongoDB server version

 5.0.26

### Typescript version (if applicable)

4.8.4

### Description

Calling insertMany() with ordered=false and rawResult=false silently drops the per-document validation errors. You only get the successfully inserted docs (or a driver error), so there’s no way to tell which documents failed validation without re-running the entire batch.

### Steps to Reproduce

const schema = new Schema({ name: { type: String, required: true }, year: Number });
const Movie = mongoose.model('Movie', schema);

await Movie.insertMany([
  { foo: 'invalid 1' },
  { name: 'valid movie' },
  { bar: 'invalid 2' }
], { ordered: false });
// Promise resolves with `[ { name: 'valid movie' } ]`
// but there is no information about the two invalid docs

### Expected Behavior

Even when rawResult=false, the resolved docs (or thrown error) should expose mongoose.validationErrors/results so callers can identify which indexes failed validation, similar to the rawResult behavior.

issues.title: Add copy-to-clipboard button for code blocks in Flask documentation.
issues.body:
### Description

I propose to add a "copy to clipboard" button next to all code blocks in the Flask documentation. This will allow users to easily copy example code or command-line snippets with a single click instead of manually selecting the text. This feature greatly improves usability and convenience, especially on mobile devices.

Flask documentation uses Sphinx for static site generation, and there is an existing Sphinx extension called [sphinx-copybutton](https://sphinx-copybutton.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) that provides this functionality out of the box. It can be enabled by simply adding it to the `extensions` list in the Sphinx `conf.py` file.


This extension also supports stripping common prompts (like `>>>`, `$`) from copied content, making the copied snippets cleaner.

### Problem this resolves

Currently, users have to manually select and copy code blocks, which can be error-prone and inconvenient. Adding a copy button makes the process faster and less error-prone.

### Additional notes

- The change is additive and does not affect existing documentation content or structure.
- This enhancement aligns Flask docs with modern documentation practices seen in many popular projects.

I am happy to implement this feature and create a pull request once the issue is approved.

Should work smoothly for anonymous as well as logged-in users.

Design should not block or slow down other parts of the editor interface.

This feature aligns well with other modern code editors and improves the overall user experience for the p5.js community. Previous discussions (like Issue #2513) highlight demand for this enhancement, but the editor/compiler has not yet received a direct toggle option.

issues.title: Add dark/light mode toggle for the documentation site
issues.body:
The documentation site for Helmet.js (https://helmetjs.github.io/) currently uses a bright light theme only.  
For users who prefer or work at night, or in low-light environments, a dark theme or a light/dark toggle would greatly improve readability and reduce eye strain.

### Proposed Solution  
- Add support for dark mode (or a toggle between light and dark themes).  
- Use `prefers-color-scheme` media query to detect system preference by default.  
- Provide a toggle UI element (e.g., a switch / icon) on the site header/footer so users can manually switch.  
- Ensure documentation text, code blocks, backgrounds, links maintain contrast in both modes.  
- Optional: Save user preference in `localStorage` so site remembers their choice.

### Why This Helps  
- Many developers use dark mode in their editors & system; having docs in dark mode aligns with their workflow.  
- Improves accessibility for users sensitive to bright backgrounds.  
- Enhances overall UX and shows attention to detail in the project.

### Additional Info  
- Browser: Tested in Chrome/Firefox on Windows/macOS.  
- Site build: Uses static docs (check repo for details).  
- Happy to propose a PR (CSS + minimal JS) if this enhancement is accepted.

### Checklist  
- [ ✅ ] I have read the Code of Conduct  
- [ ✅ ] I have reviewed the Contributing Guide  
- [ ✅ ] No existing issue requests this same feature (searched issues)
